title: BWXT — Defense Nuclear & SMR Manufacturing Squeeze
catalyst: BWXT received a $1.5B contract to build domestic uranium enrichment capability for US defense use, on top of a Q1 2026 backlog jump to $8.65B (+77% YoY). The company controls the only large reactor manufacturing facility in North America. Project Pele microreactor delivery on schedule for 2027, and Rep. April McClain Delaney (MD06) disclosed two BWXT purchases totaling $65k in May 2026.
thesis: BWXT is the unsexy manufacturing bottleneck for the entire US nuclear renaissance — naval reactors, defense enrichment, microreactors, and SMR components all flow through limited facilities BWXT controls. The thesis is supply-side: even if every utility, hyperscaler, and DoD program orders nuclear infrastructure tomorrow, BWXT's manufacturing constraints set the pace. The market frames BWXT as a defense contractor (which it is) but underweights the optionality from being the manufacturing chokepoint for the broader SMR/restart wave.

source.title: BWXT Q1 Deep Dive: Backlog Growth and U.S. Nuclear Expansion Strategies Drive Outlook

what_would_promote: (a) A second hyperscaler-adjacent BWXT contract announcement (utility-scale reactor component supply) — would shift this from defense-narrative to AI-power-narrative; (b) confirmation that Project Pele delivery holds 2027 timeline; (c) Senate or House defense committee member disclosed BWXT purchase clustering with the Delaney buys.
checks_remaining: Need a second primary source on the enrichment contract beyond the Q1 deck; need trade construction grounded in current $216 stock and 41.9x forward P/E (not currently cheap); need to differentiate from existing ai-power-bottleneck.json (which covers utility-scale nuclear generation, not manufacturing).

title: Drone Warfare Pivot — Iran Conflict Hardens DoD Procurement
catalyst: Operation Epic Fury (US/Israel strikes on Iran, March 2026) made Switchblade loitering munitions a daily-use weapon and pushed DoD toward multi-thousand-unit drone procurement. AeroVironment received a $117M Army contract for P550 long-range recon drones and a Switchblade 400 prototype agreement. The Ukraine deal, where one Ukrainian manufacturer alone plans 3M drones in 2026 vs. US 2025 production of just 300k, signals what scaled procurement looks like.
thesis: The Iran conflict has resolved a multi-year DoD debate about whether loitering munitions are a niche capability or a core ground combat platform — the answer is clearly the latter. This pulls forward $5-10B of annual procurement that previously sat in the 5-year defense plan as 'study programs.' AVAV (incumbent loitering munitions) and KTOS (target drones, Valkyrie collaborative combat aircraft) are the cleanest US-listed pure-plays. The catch: AVAV just lost a $1.4B SCAR satellite contract that triggered a triple-downgrade, so timing the entry matters more than the thesis direction.

source.title: Why The Iran Conflict Is A Structural Inflection Point For AeroVironment

what_would_promote: (a) FY2027 DoD budget request line items for loitering munitions disclosed (Feb 2027) — concrete program-of-record evidence; (b) AVAV next earnings (Sep 2026) showing book-to-bill > 1.5 and backlog growth absorbing the SCAR loss; (c) Ukraine-US bilateral drone agreement formally signed; (d) Senate Armed Services Committee disclosed cluster purchases in either name.
checks_remaining: Need a second primary source (Pentagon FY27 budget request, AVAV 10-Q, or DoD contract award) beyond press coverage; need to construct entry zones that account for AVAV post-SCAR volatility and KTOS Valkyrie production timeline; need clearer differentiation between AVAV (loitering munitions) and KTOS (autonomous fighters) given different cost-to-revenue conversion paths.

title: Quantum Computing Utility-Scale Buildout
catalyst: PsiQuantum broke ground June 17, 2026 on the world's first utility-scale, fault-tolerant quantum computer at Moreton Bay, Queensland, backed by $940M (AUD) in Australian/Queensland government commitments. IonQ separately delivered a 256-qubit trapped-ion system to Ireland's Horizon Quantum Holdings (Jun 11), launched Clavis XG Multiplex with ID Quantique for quantum key distribution (Jun 17), and is guiding to $225-245M 2026 revenue (vs. $90M-ish in 2024). HPE announced eight quantum hardware partnerships (Jun 15) including IonQ, Rigetti, Quantinuum, Intel, IQM, QuEra.
thesis: Two narratives are converging in mid-2026. First, government-funded utility-scale quantum is moving from PowerPoint to construction — PsiQuantum (private) is the most concrete proof, but every listed quantum name benefits from the validation. Second, hardware-software stack integration (HPE's eight-vendor partnership) signals that systems integrators are committing roadmaps, which forces enterprise CIOs to budget pilot dollars. IonQ is the cleanest public listing for the trapped-ion stack with real revenue traction, but the entire complex (IONQ, RGTI, QBTS) re-rates together on any major commercial milestone.

source.title: PsiQuantum Breaks Ground in Australia on Site of World's First Utility-Scale Quantum Computer

what_would_promote: (a) IonQ announces a paying enterprise contract above $10M annual recurring value (vs. current government-heavy revenue base); (b) any of the three listed quantum names (IONQ, RGTI, QBTS) reports actual logical-qubit milestones rather than physical-qubit counts; (c) DoD or DOE awards a quantum-specific procurement contract to a US-listed firm; (d) HPE or another hyperscaler discloses a commercial quantum offering with named SLAs.
checks_remaining: Need a primary source (10-Q, customer contract, government program announcement) supporting the commercialization narrative — currently most evidence is press releases and partnership announcements; need real trade construction including position sizing that accounts for 70%+ daily IV; need to articulate a falsifiable invalidation that doesn't depend on PsiQuantum (a private competitor).

title: On Holding — Insider Cluster Signals Premium Athletic Momentum
catalyst: Three insiders purchased $6.6M of ONON stock in the last 30 days, clustered within a tight window. Open SEC Form 4 filings on a name screening 74 (HOLD) in IOWN — a Growth-sleeve premium athletic brand with double-digit revenue growth, expanding into apparel and lifestyle. No identified news catalyst in the prior 14 days, which raises the quality of the signal per Step 3a heuristics.
thesis: Premium athletic apparel is undergoing share rotation: NKE/UA losing performance-runner mindshare to ONON and HOKA (HOKA-parent Deckers). ONON's gross margin expansion and DTC mix shift have outpaced sell-side estimates for four consecutive quarters. An unprompted insider cluster on a name at $0.74 of fair value (IOWN) suggests management sees runway the market is missing. This is the kind of 'no-news cluster buy' that Step 3 flags as a HIGH-QUALITY signal.

source.title: OpenInsider — ON Holding cluster purchases (3 insiders, $6.6M)

what_would_promote: ONON Q2 2026 earnings (typically mid-August) confirming gross margin trajectory and DTC growth at or above guidance, AND analyst upward revisions across at least 3 sell-side desks. That would convert the insider signal into an On Our Radar-grade catalyst with primary sources beyond the Form 4s themselves.

title: TKO Group — UFC/WWE Rights Cycle Insider Cluster
catalyst: Four TKO insiders purchased a combined $5.5M of stock in the last 30 days, including multiple directors. TKO's UFC media rights with ESPN expire in 2025-2026 and WWE Raw/SmackDown rights with Netflix/USA are entering renewal windows in 2026-2027. Live sports rights pricing has compounded mid-teens per renewal cycle since 2018; both UFC and WWE are scarcity assets in the streaming-fragmented landscape.
thesis: Live sports rights are the most defensible content category as streaming consolidates. UFC has demonstrated 10-15x rights-fee step-ups in prior cycles (Fox → ESPN was ~$1.5B over 7 years). The next UFC US deal — currently in negotiation per multiple trade reports — is widely expected to set a record. A cluster of 4 directors buying ahead of rights-cycle visibility is the kind of insider behavior that historically precedes step-function revenue inflections at media/sports holdcos.

title: IFF — $32M Insider Cluster at Specialty Chemicals Trough
catalyst: Two IFF insiders bought $32.3M of stock in early June 2026 — the largest single-name insider dollar value in the openinsider 14-day cluster screen. The buying coincides with year three of the post-DuPont N&B integration, where management has guided to a 2026 portfolio-rationalization completion and margin recovery from the current -3.3% profit margin / -2.6% ROE trough.
thesis: Insiders are voting capital, not just compensation, at what appears to be the cyclical low for a category the market has written off. IFF is the #1 global player in flavors and #2 in fragrances — categories where customer switching costs are high and global brand penetration requires presence across all three of its segments (flavors, fragrances, health & biosciences). The trough margin reflects integration drag and divestiture noise, not category economics. If the 2026 portfolio carve-out completes and margins recover toward the legacy IFF 15%+ EBITDA level, the equity re-rates substantially from 15.2x forward P/E. The $32M insider buy is the cleanest near-term signal that management believes the inflection is near.

source.title: OpenInsider Latest Cluster Buys — IFF $32.3M, 2 insiders, trade date 2026-06-01

what_would_promote: (1) Second primary source — Q2 earnings transcript or 8-K confirming portfolio rationalization timeline. (2) Confirmation buyers are CEO/CFO-level (current scrape doesn't reveal role). (3) Two consecutive quarters of expanding gross margin or sequential revenue stabilization.
checks_remaining: Step 5 High Conviction: needs 2+ primary sources, falsifiable dated catalyst (currently general 'inflection' without specific event), full trade construction. Step 5 On Our Radar: needs 1 primary source + credible secondary, trade construction even if approximate.

Parsons just reported a record $9.3B backlog and 10.1% Q1 2026 adjusted EBITDA margin — both up sequentially. IOWN overall_score 71 (HOLD).

DoD AI, cyber, and missile-defense modernization are funded under multi-year IDIQ vehicles that don't expire with CR resets. PSN's backlog now covers ~1.7x trailing revenue and the EBITDA margin expansion shows higher-margin federal mix is winning. A 4-insider cluster buy at this point in the cycle is consistent with management seeing backlog-to-revenue conversion that consensus is too cautious on. If FY2026 federal appropriations land within 4 months without sequestration, the backlog converts and the multiple expands toward defense-prime levels.

title: CDW — AI Hardware Pull-Forward Demand at the VAR Layer
catalyst: CDW reported Q1 2026 net sales of $5.68B (+9.2% YoY) on May 6, with management explicitly citing customers 'pulling forward hardware orders ahead of price increases and supply issues' driven by AI infrastructure modernization demand. On May 27, two CDW insiders cluster-purchased $2.5M of common stock — the first sizable insider buy since 2023.
thesis: Every framework we have for the AI buildout (chips, memory, grid, optics) skips the VAR layer that actually integrates and ships the gear to non-hyperscaler enterprises. CDW is the largest US technology solutions provider serving SMB, mid-market, education, and government — segments where AI deployment is just now starting, lagging hyperscaler capex by 18-24 months. The pull-forward demand language plus insider buying suggests Q2 visibility has firmed up; if the AI investment cycle is broadening beyond the Mag-7 customer set, CDW is the cleanest enterprise-AI proxy at a much more reasonable multiple than NVDA, AVGO, or AMD. Counter: CDW is a low-margin distributor with structural pricing pressure; the AI tailwind has to outweigh that drag for a sustained re-rating.

source.title: CDW Q1 Earnings Meet, Revenues Rise Y/Y on Infrastructure & AI Adoption

what_would_promote: (a) Q2 2026 print showing AI-attached hardware revenue growing >20% segmented and management raising FY guidance, OR (b) the insider buying continuing into June-July at higher dollar values, OR (c) channel checks confirming enterprise-AI deployments accelerating beyond the early pilot phase.
checks_remaining: (1) Need a primary source beyond IR press release — earnings call transcript with management's specific commentary on AI segment sizing and pipeline; (2) need to verify the cluster isn't options-related (Form 4 reading); (3) trade construction requires a view on whether the multiple compresses on margin pressure or expands on AI mix.

title: Sulfuric Acid Crunch Re-prices Sulfide Copper
catalyst: Chinese sulfuric acid exports to Chile collapsed to zero in March 2026 (vs. 151,268 t in March 2025) as China protects domestic fertilizer feedstock and Middle East seaborne sulfur supply tightens; spot acid CFR Mejillones doubled to $380/mt by mid-April. About one-fifth of global copper is extracted via sulfuric-acid SX-EW heap leaching, predominantly in Chile, so the squeeze caps Chilean copper output even before mine-side issues bite.
thesis: The shortage rewards integrated sulfide producers that smelt their own concentrate (and generate sulfuric acid as a byproduct) and penalizes SX-EW heap-leach operations dependent on imported acid. SCCO is the cleanest expression: ~80% of production comes from Peruvian sulfide ore processed through company-owned smelters at Ilo and Mexico, where the acid plants are net sellers into a regional market that just doubled. This is a supply-side substitution mechanic distinct from the structural-demand thesis behind copper-datacenter-demand (FCX) — same commodity, different driver, different ticker.

source.title: No quick sulfuric acid fix for Chilean copper sector

what_would_promote: A second primary source quantifying SCCO's net sulfuric acid sales position (10-K disclosure or IR commentary); evidence Chilean SX-EW peers (FCX Arizona, Antofagasta) guiding production cuts for 2H 2026; entry zone grounded in current price (SCCO HOLD-rated, IOWN 71).
checks_remaining: Need 2 primary sources for promotion to High Conviction; trade construction (entry/target/stop) not defined; counter-thesis (acid prices mean-revert on Mid-East ceasefire) not stress-tested.

title: Seagate — HAMR Nearline Storage Sold Out Through 2026
catalyst: Mozaic 4 (44TB HAMR) entered revenue shipments late March 2026 and is qualified by every major US hyperscaler. Production capacity for 30TB+ drives is reportedly sold out through calendar 2026 under build-to-order contracts that give 3-4 quarters of demand visibility. Next 10-Q expected late July 2026.
thesis: The active HBM/DRAM opp (MU + SNDK) captures the high-velocity AI memory leg, but the warm/cold storage leg — where the bulk of training data, checkpoints, and inference cache live — is a separate constrained market. Seagate is one of two viable suppliers, and HAMR is the only technology that scales to the $/TB hyperscalers need at the 40-50TB tier. The angle is storage tier (nearline, not HBM) and technology moat (HAMR-only at this capacity), so it's mechanism-distinct from the active memory opps.

source.title: Seagate (STX) Had a Blowout Q3. The AI Storage Bull Case Is Building

what_would_promote: Promote to On Our Radar after the next 10-Q confirms Mozaic 4 revenue contribution, nearline mix shift, and HAMR-driven gross margin expansion. Promote to High Conviction if a named hyperscaler discloses a multi-year supply agreement at the 40TB+ tier.
checks_remaining: Need at least one direct primary source (Seagate 10-Q/8-K or hyperscaler disclosure) confirming Mozaic 4 ramp — current source is TipRanks summary, which counts as secondary. Verify no overlap with SNDK in the active dram-hbm-shortage opp (confirmed: SNDK is NAND, STX is HAMR HDD — different storage tier).

catalyst: Co-CEOs Robert Duggan and Maky Zanganeh each purchased 3.81M shares ($49.99M each, ~$100M combined) on June 12, 2026, at $13.12. Ivonescimab BLA for first-line NSCLC is under FDA review with a November 2026 PDUFA date. HARMONi-6 Phase III showed ivonescimab+chemo cut risk of death by 34% vs tislelizumab combos in squamous NSCLC; data published in The Lancet and highlighted at ASCO 2026.

Summit owns ex-Greater China rights to ivonescimab from Akeso; approval would establish it as a credible PD-1/VEGF bispecific franchise. The screener flags SMMT as WATCH (IOWN 43), reflecting pre-revenue biotech risk — but the asymmetry comes from validated late-stage data plus a regulatory inflection, not from current fundamentals.

title: Amrize (AMRZ) — North American Cement for the Data Center Buildout
catalyst: 4-insider cluster buy ($2.7M) at Amrize surfaced in openinsider on 2026-06-15. AMRZ is the recently spun-out (June 2025) North American cement, aggregates and building envelope business of Holcim, led by former Holcim CEO Jan Jenisch as Chairman and CEO. Hyperscaler 2026 capex was revised UP this quarter to ~$630-650B (Microsoft $190B, Alphabet $180-190B), and each GW of new data center capacity consumes thousands of tonnes of cement for foundations, vaults and pads.
thesis: Amrize is a pureplay on US-led building materials demand at the exact moment the AI data center buildout is driving the largest non-residential construction wave in two decades. Insider buying from a fresh public-company management team with credibility (Jenisch ran Holcim 2017-2024 to outperformance) suggests they see the cement order book firming up. This is a complementary angle to the active `data-center-grid-buildout` and `ai-power-bottleneck` opps — the cement layer underneath the steel-and-copper layer those cover. IOWN screener rates AMRZ HOLD/66, sleeve Growth.

title: Qatar Helium Shortage → Industrial Gas Pricing Power (LIN)
catalyst: Qatar produces 30-38% of global helium as a byproduct of LNG processing at Ras Laffan; the Strait of Hormuz blockade since February 2026 has effectively closed the only export route for Western markets, with Iranian strikes also damaging Qatari LNG facilities. Spot helium prices have doubled and chip-grade helium inventories at South Korean fabs (Samsung, SK Hynix) sit at roughly six months of cover, after which yields are at risk.
thesis: Linde holds approximately six months of global helium demand in storage and operates the largest helium sourcing network outside the Gulf (Wyoming, Algeria, Russia subsidiaries), giving it asymmetric leverage to a sustained spot-price spike. Unlike a pure semiconductor or chemical play, Linde monetizes the shortage on both inventory revaluation and locked-in contract repricing as legacy customer deals roll over. The semiconductor end-market exposure also dovetails with our existing DRAM/HBM shortage and AI infrastructure theses — the same Hormuz catalyst that constrains memory chip supply now also constrains the helium needed to fabricate them.

source.title: Qatar helium shutdown puts chip supply chain on a two-week clock — SK hynix forced to diversify after 30% of global supply removed from the market

supporting_sources.title: Iran war cuts off helium from Qatar, and shortages will start to bite in a few weeks, threatening chip supply chains that fuel the AI boom
Beyond oil: 9 commodities impacted by the Strait of Hormuz crisis

what_would_promote: Promotion to On Our Radar requires: (1) Linde Q2 2026 earnings (July 31, 2026) confirming helium pricing pulled through to results — segment commentary specifying realized helium price uplift and locked-in contract repricing; (2) a primary-source-quality breakdown of Linde's helium revenue exposure as a % of total (currently inferred from industry coverage, not disclosed in segment reporting); (3) trade construction with concrete entry/target/stop bounded by current-market data.
checks_remaining: Need (a) at least one more primary source — ideally a Linde IR filing or 10-Q segment disclosure quantifying helium price/volume — to meet On Our Radar's 1-primary-source bar; (b) bounded trade construction; (c) a clearer view of how much of the spot-price spike is being captured by Linde vs. pass-through to end customers under existing contracts.

title: Autodesk — Full-Officer Cluster Buy at $239
catalyst: Per openinsider.com cluster screener on 2026-06-19: Autodesk insiders purchased a combined ~$993K over 1 day. Buyers span the company's senior operating roles — EVP, CFO, President, and CEO — all four C-suite/operating leaders accumulating personal stake in the same window. While modest in dollar size, the breadth of titles (every primary officer category) is the strongest possible role-quality signal for a $52B-market-cap name.
thesis: Autodesk is a $7.2B-revenue dominant design platform in architecture, engineering, construction, and manufacturing software, with 33% FCF margin and 18% revenue growth, screening at IOWN 70 (HOLD) — a 'great business at a fair price' profile rather than a deep-discount cyclical. CEO + CFO + President + EVP all buying personal shares on the same trading window typically signals (a) management views the stock as undervalued at current levels, (b) an internally-visible catalyst (Construction Cloud monetization, AI-feature pricing, Q2 guidance) the market hasn't yet priced, or (c) coordinated optics ahead of an upcoming capital allocation announcement (buyback, dividend, M&A). At 70 IOWN with a Growth-sleeve quality profile, this is worth watching for a re-rating trigger.

source.title: Insider cluster buys, openinsider.com screener

what_would_promote: Identify the specific catalyst driving the cluster: (a) imminent earnings beat or guide-up, (b) AI-monetization disclosure at Autodesk University, (c) buyback authorization. Once a falsifiable catalyst date is on the calendar, a confirmation via Autodesk IR press release or SEC Form 4 filings would clear the On Our Radar bar.

title: FISV — Activist-Backed CEO Reset After 70% Drawdown, 6-Insider Cluster
catalyst: On June 15, 2026, Fiserv announced Takis Georgakopoulos as new CEO, succeeding Mike Lyons (who left for Truist) — Simply Wall St reports the change followed activist pressure. The company simultaneously reaffirmed FY26 guidance of $8.00-$8.30 adjusted EPS and launched a $2.75B senior-note tender. Two days later (June 17), CFO Paul Todd bought 10,060 shares for ~$500K at $49.70, joining a 6-insider cluster including two directors and the Chief Administrative Officer.
thesis: FISV trades at ~$50 — roughly 71% below its July 2025 high of $175 and ~6.2x reaffirmed FY26 EPS — yet the company just delivered an activist-pushed leadership change to a JPM-payments veteran, reaffirmed guidance the market already feared was being cut, and watched its CFO commit half a million dollars of personal capital. Six insiders buying in a week is the strongest internal signal in 18 months for a mega-cap payments name. If the new CEO's first 100-day plan articulates a credible Clover/AI commerce reacceleration and the activist confirms continued board presence, the multiple compression unwinds toward a 10-12x forward P/E — a 60-95% re-rate before any earnings improvement.

source.title: Why Fiserv (FISV) Stock Is Up Today

what_would_promote: Confirmation of which activist drove the CEO change (e.g., 13D filing or named letter), a primary source from the Q2 earnings call (late July) where Georgakopoulos lays out a strategy reset, and openinsider confirming the cluster widens to include the new CEO. Promotion to On Our Radar after Q2 print if guidance is reiterated and a credible Clover/AI commerce reacceleration narrative emerges.
checks_remaining: No 2nd primary source yet — the SEC 8-K and the Form 4 filings exist but a named activist + clear strategic plan are missing. The 'falling knight' risk is acute (FISV could grind lower if the merchant-acquiring business is structurally impaired). Trade construction would need to model normalized earnings power under the new strategy.

title: QNT — 10-Insider, $24.6M Cluster Buy Six Weeks Post-IPO
catalyst: Quantinuum (QNT) priced its IPO at $60 on June 4, 2026 ($1.68B raised — largest quantum IPO in history); openinsider as of June 21 shows a 10-insider cluster buy totaling $24.6M with trades clustered around June 13-18 at prices near $69-70. The buyer mix includes board members and senior executives — unusually broad for any sub-$10B name, let alone a recent IPO where customary lockups normally suppress filings.
thesis: The standard post-IPO pattern is insider selling once lockups roll off; a $24.6M, 10-person CLUSTER BUY within six weeks of pricing is the opposite signal and is the kind of management-conviction event that historically precedes positive earnings revisions or contract announcements. The mechanism is distinct from the broader quantum-utility-scale-buildout thesis already in stalking: this is a name-specific insider-conviction setup, not a sector re-rate. Quantinuum's $100M CHIPS Act award and Honeywell/CQH 82% post-IPO ownership mean any incremental commercial win flows almost entirely to the public float.

source.title: OpenInsider — QNT 10-Insider Cluster Buy ($24.6M, June 13-18, 2026)

what_would_promote: (a) An 8-K or press release identifying the catalyst the insiders are buying ahead of (typical pattern: a contract announcement or roadmap milestone within 90 days); (b) confirmation that Honeywell or Cambridge Quantum is participating in the cluster (would imply parent-level conviction, not just employee-level); (c) a primary source from Quantinuum confirming Q3 customer pipeline data above prior commentary.
checks_remaining: Need a falsifiable catalyst date — currently the trigger is just 'wait and see what they were buying.' Trade construction is meaningless without an entry framework on a name that has only traded for ~3 weeks. Need to verify the cluster isn't artificially inflated by single-large-insider noise (manual SEC Form 4 review). Theme overlap with existing 'quantum-utility-scale-buildout' stalking entry: similar sector exposure but the angle (insider-conviction event vs. utility-scale construction validation) is materially different.

title: Crane NXT CEO+CFO Insider Cluster at 9.9x Forward P/E
catalyst: Crane NXT CEO Aaron Saak purchased 24,000 shares for ~$1.01M open-market on June 12, 2026 (his largest open-market buy as CEO; brings his stake to 64,595 shares). CFO Christina Cristiano added in the same window, making this a 2-officer cluster totaling ~$1.16M.
thesis: CXT is the post-spinoff industrial-security / banknote-authentication platform that has rolled up OpSec, De La Rue, and Antares Vision. The CEO and CFO are buying at 9.9x forward P/E into the back half of a 17% revenue growth year with management guiding $4.10-$4.40 adjusted EPS for FY2026. Open-market officer buys at decade-low valuations during integration windows have historically preceded re-ratings — and the screener's AI Resilience score of 7 confirms the authentication-platform business is structurally insulated from generative-AI commoditization.

what_would_promote: Crane NXT files Q2 (calendar) earnings with the Antares Vision integration delivering >10% organic growth and reaffirming the $4.10-$4.40 EPS range, OR insider buying broadens to a third officer/director within 30 days, OR shares close above the post-purchase $50 level and hold for 2 weeks.

title: Hamilton Lane — Private-Markets Platform with Insider Cluster Vote
catalyst: A 3-insider cluster on Hamilton Lane (HLNE) totaling $14.2M settled on 2026-06-11, with the screener carrying the stock at an overall score of 70 (HOLD on the cusp of BUY). The buy lands as Evergreen private-markets product AUM is compounding double-digit and management has guided to continued fee-rate expansion in the back half of FY26.
thesis: Hamilton Lane is the rare alt-asset platform that combines best-in-class fundamentals (32.4% ROE, 32.8% profit margin, 24.3% D/E) with a sub-market valuation (Forward P/E 11.1x). The Evergreen platform — semi-liquid private-markets vehicles aimed at HNW intermediated channels — is a structural growth engine outside the institutional consultancy core, and insider buying at $14M alongside a 70-score screener report suggests management sees the public-market discount as cyclical rather than structural. The angle the manifest does not currently cover is the 'shovels for the private-markets gold rush' framing.

source.title: OpenInsider cluster-buys list 2026-06-24 (HLNE 3-insider cluster, $14.24M)

what_would_promote: A second confirming signal — either (a) consensus FY26 EPS revision higher after the next quarterly print, (b) a major HNW-channel distribution win for the Evergreen platform, or (c) a published primary source (industry report or company IR deck) quantifying Evergreen run-rate fees enough to underwrite an entry zone and 12-month target.
checks_remaining: No primary IR/SEC source has been quantified for the Evergreen growth thesis yet; trade construction (entry, target, stop) not yet specified — both required to clear the On Our Radar bar.

title: IBP — Last Public Insulation Installer After QXO Takes TopBuild Private
catalyst: QXO's $17B acquisition of TopBuild (announced April 19, 2026; expected close Q3 2026) removes the #1 publicly-traded insulation installer from the market, leaving Installed Building Products as the only large-cap pure-play public insulation installer. Four insiders (including CFO Michael Miller buying 990 shares on June 11) made open-market purchases totaling ~$1.0M in the trailing three months, with no selling activity. Open-market insider clustering is unusual at IBP — most insider activity historically has been routine sales.
thesis: When a major comparable goes private at a strategic premium, scarcity value typically re-rates the surviving public peer. IBP is now the only $200+ stock pure-play public exposure to residential and commercial insulation installation in North America — a vertical undergoing aggressive roll-up driven by QXO and other distributor strategics seeking installer reach. The CFO/COO/Director cluster buy suggests management sees either takeout optionality or that the post-deal scarcity premium has not yet been priced in. The Berkshire-homebuilder-revaluation opp covers builders directly; this is a derivative play that benefits from the same housing-recovery thesis but with a strategic-buyer optionality kicker.

source.title: QXO to Acquire TopBuild for $17 Billion

what_would_promote: (a) A second strategic acquirer (QXO, Beacon, ABC Supply, Builders FirstSource) signals interest in IBP — would move this to On Our Radar with takeout language; (b) IBP discloses its own roll-up acceleration (M&A deal flow >2x prior cadence) on Q2 2026 earnings; (c) Housing-starts data confirms a sustainable recovery, which would make organic upside additive to the consolidation premium.
checks_remaining: Need a second primary source documenting installer-sector consolidation premium (analyst note quantifying takeout multiple); need trade construction grounded in current $218 / 19.5x forward P/E with explicit comparison to TopBuild's takeout multiple; need to ensure this doesn't conflict with the existing berkshire-homebuilder-revaluation opp (PHM, MTH) — different mechanism (installer scarcity vs. builder multiple expansion), so de-dup OK.

title: CarMax — CEO + 3-Director Cluster Buy Follows Q1 Beat & Four-Pillar Turnaround Launch
catalyst: CarMax reported Q1 FY27 on 2026-06-17 with revenue of $8.01B (vs. ~$7.39B consensus) and EPS of $1.31 (vs. ~$0.96 consensus) — the cleanest beat in ~8 quarters and the first explicit unveiling of CEO Keith Barr's 'four-pillar' strategic framework. On June 22, 2026, CEO Barr bought 9,400 shares at $53.005 (~$498K), and Directors Bensen, Chawla, and Oneil bought concurrently (~$1.24M cluster total across 4 insiders, span ≤3 days). The Form 4 cluster lands within five trading days of the beat — a 'put-the-stock-where-the-strategy-is' signal.
thesis: Used-car retail has been a value trap since the rate shock (KMX down ~50% from late-2024 highs, screener score 52 / WATCH). What's different now is the combination of (a) a fresh CEO publicly tying himself to a multi-year framework instead of cycle-managing, (b) sales momentum returning before pricing actions fully normalize gross profit per retail unit (which was -$230 YoY but stabilizing), and (c) four open-market insider buys including the CEO at $53 — historically rare for KMX directors. This is an early-cycle 'CEO conviction at the inflection' setup. The pattern's failure mode is insiders being early on a consumer-credit downturn; would only graduate to On Our Radar if Q2 confirms unit growth and CAF (CarMax Auto Finance) credit metrics hold.

source.title: CarMax Reports First Quarter Fiscal 2027 Results

what_would_promote: (a) A second open-market cluster buy (e.g., CFO or independent director) at higher prices, signaling conviction past $53; (b) Q2 FY27 confirming gross-profit-per-retail-unit stabilization at or above $2,200; (c) CAF 90-day delinquency rate flat or improving QoQ; (d) the four-pillar framework getting a quantified margin/ROIC target the market can price.
checks_remaining: Need second primary source on the four-pillar framework specifics (transcript / 10-Q to drop). Need to validate that the KMX screener reflects the post-beat numbers — current score (52) is from prior screen_date. Need trade construction grounded in current $53 area (not yet attempted at Stalking tier).

title: HF Sinclair Officer Cluster — Refiner Trough + Mid-Con Crack Spread
catalyst: HF Sinclair (DINO) saw a 2-insider cluster purchase totaling ~$1.14M on 2026-06-16, with refiner crack spreads sitting near multi-year lows following the Hormuz-driven crude price spike. Mid-continent refiners with logistical advantage (DINO's Salt Lake / Tulsa system) are at a structural cost-curve advantage when crude differentials widen.
thesis: Refiners are mid-cycle to trough on margins, while the Hormuz disruption has widened WTI vs. Brent differentials in favor of inland US refiners. DINO insider buying at this trough is consistent with capacity discipline and renewable-diesel cash generation that consensus has under-modeled. Distinct from our existing energy opps (LNG/shale upstream) — this is refining downstream.

source.title: OpenInsider cluster buys feed

what_would_promote: Mid-continent crack spreads holding above $20/bbl through Q3 2026, OR a second insider cluster on the next earnings.
